name: CUT-B billing fast-follow: drop legacy subscriptions + sweep ~18 billing/quota readers to v2 subscription + rewire quota-reset cron (carved from WI-586)
description: Orchestrator B-ruling (2026-06-16, operator-agreed): carve the riskiest cutover slice out of WI-586. subscriptions is 1 of the 5 legacy identity tables (its account_id FK is welded to legacy accounts; v2 subscription already exists, organization-keyed, CUT-B3/WI-693). WI-586 drops the 4 identity tables (accounts/profiles/consent_states/family_links) only; subscriptions drop + its reader sweep + cron rewiring land here as a focused fast-follow so the payment-metering/cron risk gets isolated review instead of riding inside an already-large identity PR. Consistent with the operator's 2026-06-11 CUT-A/CUT-B split ruling, applied one level deeper.
acceptanceCriteria: 1. ENUMERATE-FIRST: bucket ALL ~18 billing/subscriptions/quota readers (services + routes + inngest) across a/b/c -- (a) already flag-branched ternary, (b) legacy fn with a separate v2 twin reached under flag-on, (c) FLAG-ON-REACHABLE UNBRANCHED = reads legacy subscriptions under flag-on. Report the bucket. 2. FLIP-CRITICAL (must land before #8, in 586 or a flip-critical sub-item -- shepherd's placement call): every class-(c) billing reader gated to the v2 subscription helper so flag-on serves v2 (not stale legacy) data at flip. flag-off byte-identical. 3. account-repository.subscriptions->subscription repoint where callers are flag-on (organization-keyed v2 helpers already exist). 4. WIRE resetExpiredQuotaCyclesV2 into the nightly cron under flag-on (quota-reset.ts); flag-off keeps legacy. 5. quota-FK rehome: repoint quotaPools + profileQuotaUsage off legacy subscriptions onto v2 subscription BEFORE the subscriptions drop (ic-orch-009/010 precondition). 6. SUBSCRIPTIONS DROP migration (split out of WI-586's 0118 drop-4; takes the next free migration number after 586's): drops subscriptions only, no dangling FK, ## Rollback section + snapshot. POST-FLIP (before #11). 7. DROP-SAFETY PROOF: full flag-on api:test:integration against a committed-migration DB INCLUDING this subscriptions-drop migration -> billing/quota/cron suites GREEN, no 500. 8. flag-off unchanged. Distinct from WI-779 (post-cutover legacy-symbol removal).
riskImpact: P1. Touches the riskiest surface: nightly billing cron (quota-reset.ts:50 unconditionally calls legacy resetExpiredQuotaCycles; v2 twin resetExpiredQuotaCyclesV2 at trial-v2.ts:317 exists but UNWIRED -> 500s nightly post-subscriptions-drop), ACID quota metering, and the account-repository.subscriptions->subscription repoint. SEQUENCING: the subscriptions DROP is post-flip (before #11/M-DROP) and adds a SECOND operator-only destructive gate. FLIP-CRITICAL EXCEPTION: any flag-on-reachable billing reader still reading legacy subscriptions (a class-(c) site) serves STALE payment/subscription data the instant the flag flips (#8) -> that subset MUST be flag-gated to the v2 helper BEFORE #8, not deferred. Lingering legacy subscriptions = a stale-read window on a payments table; bound by running this WP immediately after WI-586.
